entry.title: Building an SQL interface from an ABAP program
entry.content:
Dear SAP all,

I am SAP HANA Database Administrator and we my colleagues, we are mananging around 100 HANA Database.

I am looking for a tool to do a HANA usera mass deployment.

Example : an employe is leaving the company and I want to desactive/remove his HANA user from our 100 HANA Databases. Actually, I do have to connect one by one to all 100 Databases and desactive/remove his HANA user.

Do you know if there is tool to start one SQL statement (DROP user Xolduser) to all 100 HANA Databases ?

There is SAP HANA Studio and SAP HANA Cockpit, but I have to connect to all 100 HANA DBs one by one.
There is the DBACOCKPIT from PSM but I also have to connect one by one to all 100 HANA DBs.
Theris is hdbsql client but I also have to connect one by one to all 100 HANA DBs

I would like to build an SQL Interface, a kind of DB02 SQL Editor or DBACOCKPIT.

My goal is :

1. I start a t-code calling a ZABAP Program

2. I create a ZTable with all DBCO connections fields

3. When I start the ZABAP Program, I get a screen and I select all HANA DB in which one I want to start a unique SQL statement.

4. When the HANA Database a choosen (filled out) from the ZABAP Program :

4.1. The DBCO connection are created to connect all selected/choosen HANA Databases

4.2. The SQL statement is executed in all selected/choosen HANA Databases.

The goal is really to avoid connecting to all 100 HANA Databases and start, one by one, the same SQL statement. This is why I am looking for a tool to do HANA DB user mass deployment or to build a ZABAP program to created DBCO connexions and execute a SQL statement in all 100 HANA DB from a unique screen.

Do you already have build that kind of ABAP program ?
Or do you know a tool to do HANA DB user mass deployment ?

Thanks to ALL !

Best regards
PC

entry.title: EC Time LOA only for active and inactive headcount vs. paid or unpaid leave
entry.content: We currently use EC Time for LOA tracking only mainly for the US but other global locations would like to start using it too. Based on the location, they could be in a paid leave or unpaid leave and at the same time need to be considered active or inactive for headcount reporting. For example, in the US someone could be on an unpaid personal leave and would still be considered active in headcount, but in Europe an unpaid personal leave would be considered inactive in headcount reporting. We had a ticket open with support but was told we could only use employee statuses paid or unpaid. Does anyone else have the same sort of requirement? We also replicate minimal employee/org data back to ECC for downstream business purposes. Time is not managed in EC for any of our locations because they use outside vendors/processes for time/attendance.

entry.title: How does depreciation calculation in Asset Accounting differ between SAP ECC 6.0 EHP6/7 and S/4?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts in Asset Accounting within Financial Accounting,

In the relevant literature, it is recommended to activate the new FI-AA directly when implementing Asset Accounting in SAP for the first time. This not only provides an extended range of functionalities but also reduces the effort required for the future migration to S/4HANA. While certain activities are still necessary during the migration, their scope is significantly smaller if the new Asset Accounting has already been used in SAP ECC.

The advantages of the new Asset Accounting can be summarized as follows:

  • Real-time postings: All depreciation areas are updated in real time.
  • Flexible leading valuation: There is no hierarchical dependency— all depreciation areas are now treated equally.
  • Parallel document creation: Separate documents are posted for each valuation, ensuring correct values from the outset.
  • Simplified valuation plan: The structure and handling of valuations have been optimized.
  • New transaction for valuation-specific postings: This allows targeted restrictions to a specific ledger group or depreciation area.
  • Elimination of delta depreciation areas: Parallel valuation is now performed without the need for separate delta depreciation areas.
  • No restriction of transaction types to depreciation areas: Transaction types can now be used flexibly across all depreciation areas.
  • Different fiscal year variants per valuation: Only the start and end dates of the fiscal year must match.
  • Simplified handling of different accounting principles: Assets can be capitalized according to one accounting principle while being expensed under another.

 

I am interested in the differences in depreciation processing across the three systems: SAP ECC 6.0 up to EHP6, SAP ECC 6.0 EHP7 and later, and SAP S/4HANA.

I have noticed that in transaction AFAB, the reason for the posting run (Planned posting run, Repeat, Restart, Unplanned posting run) is missing in SAP S/4HANA.

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,
Emal Rahman

entry.title: International Editable SALV Day 2025...
entry.content:
International Editable SALV Day 2025 – Year 17

 

Dear CL_SALV_TABLE Fans,

Welcome to February 8th, 2025, which is the seventeenth International Editable SALV Day. See below for a link to a blog I wrote to celebrate this day exactly one year ago.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/international-editable-salv-day-2024-year-16/ba-p/13598749

This day marks the 17h anniversary of James Hawthorne going cap in hand to SAP and suggesting maybe the CL_SALV_TABLE could be brought up to functional parity with the CL_GUI_ALV_GRID and have an option to be editable.

https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/cl-salv-table-needs-editable-functionality-sap-please-provide-this/m-p/3372776

Each year it gets more difficult to write this blog because there is never anything new to say.

In the UK we have the saying “banging you head against a brick wall” which alludes to doing something repeatedly which can have no chance if success, but you do it anyway, because you are so frustrated.

In VIZ comic there was once a character called “Sammy Smalls – he runs into Walls!” who said, “Like a rocket round the sun, look at wall, here I come!” and the ran into the wall at top speed. The wall was 100% undamaged, he was not.

Now, just to make this even more futile I am posting this on the SAP Community Website – which no-one ever visits anymore.

The SAP GUI is not going anywhere. You may have noticed SAP just extended the life of ECC 6.0 by three more years, albeit with conditions. And even when an organization goes onto S/4HANA often they still use the SAP GUI because people like it. They have moaned about it non-stop for thirty years, but they like it really.

It has been argued that Fiori apps which have one single purpose are the way forward, but in real life end users tend to like SAP GUI applications with then million buttons. You can pretend that is not true, but you would be lying to yourself. Take Excel or Word for example and look at how many options they have.

Now, I am just going to repeat what I said last year, word for word.

It is as simple as this.

  • You get asked to write a read-only ALV report.
  • It is faster and simpler to do this using CL_SALV_TABLE than CL_GUI_ALV_GRID because you do not have to mess around with screens and controls and what-not.
  • Indeed, SAP recommend using CL_SALV_TABLE for all new developments of this nature.
  • The report goes live. The users start asking for extra things, as always.
  • They want some custom functions at the top of the screen.
  • They want to be able to change some of the fields and have that replicate to the database.
  • Well, you are right out of luck. You cannot do either with CL_SALV_TABLE.
  • There are workarounds for both, but they are scary, because SAP keeps finding ways to shut those workarounds down, so such an approach is not future proof.
  • The only viable option is to start again, this time using CL_GUI_ALV_GRID.
  • Then the next time you get a request for a “read-only” report the “once bitten twice shy” thing kicks in and you start with CL_GUI_ALV_GRID from the get-go.

I like CL_SALV_TABLE and so do a lot of SAP developers. But everyone likes having the editability feature a lot more. But it’s never going to happen. As has been mentioned you would think that if 100% of your customers want something you might consider giving it to them, but obviously not.

Very soon – maybe already - we will get to the stage some people reading this blog will be younger than the age of the request to make the SALV editable. Since the SAP GUI is not going anywhere (evidenced by the huge amount of development money pumped into every year by SAP) it is possible I will die of old age before the request is actioned. I am 56 already.

Nevertheless, I will keep going. Miracles can happen.

Until then the situation is “Oi you 31,000 SAP ERP Customers! You see this? You like this? You want this? Well, you can’t have it!”

 

See you next year!

Cheersy Cheers

Paul

entry.title: Re: Logic to get task list operations on the basis of equ...
entry.content:
hello @Badalsatyam ,

You can refer EAPL, PLKO and PLPO tables. 

Pass the EQUNR in table EAPL, PLNTY = 'E' and get the task list Group (PLNNR) and Group counter (PLNAL). Then pass Group (PLNNR), Group counter (PLNAL) and fetch header details such as description, Plant.

Then pass PLNNR, PLNAL into table PLPO and fetch the operation details such as control key (STEUS), Operation text (LTXA1), work center (ARBID) etc.

Vaibhav Mishra

entry.title: Re: Priority in MB26
entry.content:
hello @Schorsch8 ,

1. You can try with requirement date in reservation number. Maintenance order basic start date is updated as requirement date in reservation. This date will be used in PR and then in PO. So as per the requirement date material should be issued.

2. You can request user to maintain a higher reorder level for the material in material master in MRP view.

3. You can trigger an email when GR is posted against the PO to the department members. But here everyone will get intimated about GR so anyone can grab the material. Try to control by discipline.

Vaibhav Mishra

entry.title: Chain the General Splitters in CPI iFlow
entry.content:
Hello CPI folks,

Recently I faced a support case that my customer is using a chained General Splitter (3  to be specific) design in their iFlow integration. This caused an unexpected result from the test.

I did some test with similar scenario to understand the behavior behind the scene. It appears that this was not documented anywhere before. Hence I would like to share the result from my test to explain how the General Splitter works in a chained situation.

The SAP standard document about General Splitter is here https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/define-general-splitter

The General Splitter splits a composite message comprising N messages into N individual messages, each containing 1 message with the enveloping elements of the composite message. The term enveloping elements refers to these elements including the split point. Note elements that follow the one that is indicated as split point in the original message (but on the same level), aren't counted as enveloping elements. They won't be part of the resulting messages.

As mentioned above, in most cases we will use General Splitter as a 'ForEach' iterator to loop from a large XML payload to extract each element and process it individually because your API may only able to deal with one element at a time. But if your API supports to process elements in batch, you can set the 'grouping' in your General Splitter so it can help to significantly reduce the overhead with many API calls to the backend.

Let's take one example payload here that I used to test this scenario:

 

 

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<root>
    <parent>
        <child>
            <item id="1">1</item>
        </child>
        <child>
            <item id="2">2</item>
        </child>
        <child>
            <item id="3">3</item>
        </child>
    </parent>
    <parent>
        <child>
            <item id="4">4</item>
        </child>
        <child>
            <item id="5">5</item>
        </child>
        <child>
            <item id="6">6</item>
        </child>
    </parent>
    <parent>
        <child>
            <item id="7">7</item>
        </child>
        <child>
            <item id="8">8</item>
        </child>
        <child>
            <item id="9">9</item>
        </child>
    </parent>
</root>

 

 

 

I want to use two Splitters to firstly split at parent to do something, and then split the child under each parent to process it. It will look like below JavaScript code logic if you don't set any grouping.

 

 

 

Parents.forEach( (Parent) => {
    //<do something with parent>
    Parent.Childen.forEach( (Child) => {
        //<do something with child>
    })
})

 

 

 

Just assuming that my API can process parents in batch. Then I could set the grouping to "2" so that two parents will be processed by API within single call. The logic will look like following. (NOTE: There's no forEachTwo method available in JS)

 

 

 

Parents.forEachTwo( (TwoParents) => {
    //<do something with two parents>
    TwoParents.getChilden().forEach( (Child) => {
        //<do something with child>
    })
})

 

 

 

 Here is the iFlow design for this scenario:

As usual, I use Timer upon deployment to trigger this flow on demand. And I use Content Modifier to set the payload into message Body. After each step I use a groovy script to log the message payload.

The setting for first parent Splitter is like following (Grouping = 2)

The setting for second child Splitter (Grouping = <blank>)

Ideally we shall see

1) 2 splits from parents (2 parents in first split + 1 parent in second split)
2) 6 child splits from first group parent (2*3), 3 child splits from the last parent (1*3).

So the groovy logger (after 2nd splitter) shall be called 9 times (we have 9 children in total) as we expected. But that is not the case.

From the message MPL log, we can see only 5 attachments with "Header (after 2nd split)" which means the groovy script logger for child has been called only 5 times in total. (Even though the number of parent logger with "Header (after 1st split)" attachment is correct: 2 times)

Why ? Because children also have been further splitted with grouping 2. The diagram is as following:

The general splitter works in a way like this:

1) If you precisely set the grouping to "X", it will create a new header called "SapGroup" and fill the value "X" to it. (If this header already exists, it will be modified to "X")
2) If you keep the grouping setting to blank, the Splitter will firstly detect if there's any existing "SapGroup" value in the message header. If yes, it will take this value "X". If this header does not exist, it will set the default value "1" so forEach logic applies. (But bear in mind this will not create "SapGroup" header)

I can clearly see the value of this header in my logger attachment.

As you can see, even I keep the child Splitter with blank in the grouping setting. It will still take the SapGroup = 2 value from message header which was created by the parent Splitter.

The resolution to address this issue is very simple, either you precisely set the grouping to 1. (Again, this will create SapGroup = 1 header so subsequent splitter with blank grouping will be affected). Or if you want to resume the default grouping behavior for all the subsequent splitter(s), you can use Content Modifier or Groovy script to remove this "SapGroup" header before calling another Splitter in the chain.

After all, you still need to do comprehensive test to make sure that Splitter chain in your iFlow design will work as what you expect.

Hope this article can help you to better understand the behavior of this powerful buildblock in iFlow design.

Cheers
Lionel

entry.title: Re: Unlocking the Power of Version Management in SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning
entry.content:
@dedefelrodrigues  

Thanks for your engagement and insightful comments.

I'll address item 2 first: "Actuals into Actuals version."

I am the Product Manager for Rapid FP&A Suite for SAP S/4HANA, a free Business Content offering for SAP Analytics Cloud customers.

In this solution, we store Actuals in the Actual Version and also populate Actual Periods within the Plan version (we provide Data Actions to enable the process to copy Actual into Plan). Additionally, we offer two approaches to blend the Actual and Plan versions into a single Story view:

  1. Rolling Forecast Template – Used in input forms like Rapid Sales, which seamlessly blend Actuals and Plan across time.
  2. Advanced Filters – Used in input forms like Rapid Finance, which also allows a blend of Actual and Plan, but allows the Actual time periods to have a unique Audit Trail from the Plan time periods. 

Regarding the currency factor, we don't encounter the issue you noted: our Input Forms dynamically enable local currency input based on the company code in use. In Reports and Dashboards, users can toggle between local and global currency using a Currency Toggle at the top of each story. A dynamic Measure ensures accurate currency in the report body.

Finally, our standard Rapid comparison reports demonstrate how to compare any combination of Version and Time to any other combination of Version and Time, providing flexibility in financial analysis.     Bring it all together: storing Actual and Plan into Plan versions and dynamically blending Actual and Plan into a view in front of users is not an issue across the Rapid Suite.

Maybe you can download and look at this blog, which has free download instructions from SAP.   https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/implementer-s-guide-to-rapid-financial-planning-amp-analysis-for-sap-s/ba-p/13487021

entry.title: Re: Unlocking the Power of Version Management in SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning
entry.content:
@dedefelrodrigues  

I'll address item 1 next: Rolling Forecast.

In Rapid FP&A Suite for SAP S/4HANA, we support a Rolling Forecast version across all four models. Our Actual data runs through September 2024, with a Plan extending from October 2024 to December 2025 (15 months forward).

For example, consider a CFO requesting both:

  • A 2024 Reforecast (9 months of Actuals + 3 months of Plan).
  • A 2025 Budget (12 months of Plan).

We demonstrate why and how both processes can run within a single PLAN version, ensuring that any updates to the Q4 2024 Forecast flow seamlessly into the 2025 Budget.

At any stage, an SAC Admin can:

  • Copy the 2024 Plan into a Forecast2024 version.
  • Copy the 2025 Plan into a Budget2025 version.

This approach allows planners to work within one PLAN version while maintaining flexibility to split versions as needed -and when "done" with a calendar year. Notably, the same planners responsible for the 2025 Budget often handle the 2024 Re-forecast, making it critical to keep both processes aligned. Once 2025 planning is complete, planners will want to compare the full-year 2025 Budget vs. the 2024 Re-Forecast, which exists in PLAN throughout and can be versioned separately when needed.

You may have a bit more complex scenarios, but history shows that forcing planners to manage all the public versions independently inside the stories creates confusion and potential roll-up issues.

For further details and a free download of the Rapid FP&A Suite,  refer to the same blog:
Implementer’s Guide to Rapid Financial Planning

entry.title: Re: Unlocking the Power of Version Management in SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning
entry.content:
@dedefelrodrigues 

I'll address item 3 here: Private Versions.

I appreciate your perspective on this topic. We don’t need SAC models with 30 dimensions, as that would introduce unnecessary complexity.

In Rapid FP&A Suite for SAP S/4HANA, we deliver four models:

  • Rapid Finance
  • Rapid Sales
  • Rapid Workforce
  • Rapid Capital

Not all users work across all four models, though Corporate FP&A teams typically review and consolidate global assumptions.

When it comes to scenarios, there are generally two types in FP&A:

  1. Personal Scenarios – These are individual, exploratory scenarios that planners create to test assumptions before deciding. This is where Private Versions shine.
  2. Process-Driven Scenarios: These scenarios are required centrally or regionally and should be reflected in Public Versions for consistency and collaboration.

One of SAC's most exciting features is its Private Versions, especially compared to SAP’s planning history. With Private Versions, users can run what-if analyses, step away (even go to lunch!), and return to refine their work—all without committing changes to the central team. This level of flexibility wasn’t available in the past.

Additionally, SAC leverages Private Versions effectively in Predictive Modeling. When running a prediction, planners must first push the results into a Private Version, allowing them to fine-tune the prediction before committing it to a Public Version. This structured approach ensures that only validated and refined data makes its way into centrally mandated planning versions.

One more thing—if a cross-functional user or group needs to run a scenario across the entire SAC suite, they should consider requesting the setup of one or more Public Versions. The SAC Admin can even configure security settings so that only the relevant team can access that Public Version, allowing them to work end-to-end across models in a controlled environment. Later, if the scenario proves helpful, they can copy it into the active Plan Version and share it centrally with a broader audience.

Ultimately, effective modeling is about balancing structure and flexibility. Private Versions allow personalized flexibility, allowing planners to iterate, refine, and explore without disrupting centralized processes. At the same time, Public Versions ensure structured collaboration when a scenario needs to be shared across teams or the organization.

entry.title: Re: Unlocking the Power of Version Management in SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning
entry.content:
@dedefelrodrigues 

Addendum: A Common Scenario and How Rapid Suite Handles It

Let me give you a common scenario and explain how Rapid FP&A manages it efficiently:

Imagine the CFO requests three scenarios—a Base Case, High Case, and Low Case.

Rather than creating all three versions at once and making them available in every input view (which could lead to confusion), here’s a structured approach I’d recommend:

  1. All inputs are always made in the Working Plan (Plan Version).
  2. First, complete the Base Case within the Plan Version. Once finalized, we archive it to the Base Version.
  3. Next, build the Low Case by adjusting relevant assumptions in the Plan Version, and when ready, archive it to the Low Version.
  4. Finally, complete the High Case in the Plan Version and archive it to the High Version. (You can do these in any order, but this stepwise approach keeps things structured.)

Following this method, all planning activity occurs in one Working Plan (Plan Version), and a scenario is archived into its respective version only once it is finalized.

You're likely introducing complexity and inconsistency if you require planners across multiple regions and four models to input High, Low, and Base simultaneously. This is especially true when 75%-90% of the data in each version remains the same, depending on the type of scenario being tested:

  • If the scenario only adjusts revenue, operating expenses may stay the same.
  • If the scenario only adjusts operating expenses, revenue assumptions may not change.

By structuring the process in phases—always working in the Plan Version first and archiving when ready—planners stay focused, the data remains consistent, and the overall scenario-building process is more controlled and efficient.

Plus, your SAC administrators will thank you!

entry.title: LTMOM Data Migration Cockpit Error: Cannot save message to application log" during Prepare Step
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I'm encountering an issue during the "Prepare" step of an asset value posting migration using LTMOM and the Data Migration Cockpit. We've built a custom LTMOM solution around the BAPI BAPI_FIXEDASSET_OVRTAKE_POST to migrate asset postings (similar to AS92 functionality). We upload a custom data template, but the process fails at the Prepare stage with the error: "Cannot save message to application log; see long text."

The long text provides minimal information:

Diagnosis
The application tried to save a message to the application log, but some information about the message is missing. This means that the message cannot be saved to the application log. The following information is available:
Message type = E
Message ID = 
Message number = 000
System Response
Cannot save message to application log.

  • Field Mapping: I've reviewed the field mapping in LTMOM, but I'm unsure which specific fields are required for the application log itself. Could someone please provide a list of the essential fields that must be mapped for logging purposes (e.g., LOG_ID, OBJECT, MESSAGE_TYPE, MESSAGE_ID, MESSAGE_NUMBER, MESSAGE_TEXT)? Do these need to be mapped even if I'm not actively using the application log?

    Could anyone offer suggestions on what might be causing this error and how to resolve it? Any insights into the required field mappings for the application log in LTMOM would be greatly appreciated. If you've experienced a similar issue, please share your solution.

    Thanks in advance for your help!

    Regards

    Ramiwal 

    This error occurs before the BAPI is called, indicating a problem with the migration framework itself. I suspect it's related to missing or incorrect field mappings related to the application log.

entry.content:
https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/resources/sap/suite/ui/generic/template/js/AnnotationHelper-dbg.js 

I see that AnnotationHelper.js doesn't handle the annotation @ui.presentationVariant.groupBy, but does handle the annotation @ui.presentationVariant.sortOrder. This causes the annotation @ui.presentationVariant.sortOrder can work normally, and the annotation @ui.presentationVariant.groupBy  doesn't work. That's what I suspected.

But you can try it with front-end extension,  Put the following code into the ObjectPageExt.controller.js.

onBeforeRebindTableExtension: function(oRebindObject) {
    oRebindObject.getParameter("bindingParams").sorter.push(new sap.ui.model.Sorter("StorageLocation", true, true));
}

entry.title: Create Counter for same properties in dimension
entry.content:
Dear all,

I am currently working on a problem in SAC where I am looking to optimize my solution.

I have a customer with a very specific expectation. For simplification reasons let us assume the SAC model has one dimension called "User" with 2 properties "CostCenter" and "WFH Flag". The model has one measure called "Counter" which is currently blank. Now the customer wants to fill this measure, so that for each user you are able to see the count of the CostCenter and WFH Flag.

Before:

UserCost CenterWFH FlagCounter
A1Remote-
B1Remote-
C1Hybrid-
D2Office-
E2Office-
F2Office-
G3Remote-

 

After:

UserCost CenterWFH FlagCounter
A1Remote2
B1Remote2
C1Hybrid1
D2Office3
E2Office3
F2Office3
G3Remote

1

I have created this solution by looping through the user dimension, counting the concatenated string of CostCenter and Flag. Then I place it into a property of the user dimension which I write to a measure via data action.

This approach is fine if you have a few users. But with thousands of users it will take some time.

Is there a better approach for example using data action functionalities? I was thinking of having a loop in the DTAs but I did not manage to get it.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: How to set up Manage Sales Order ( Live Process Proje...
entry.content:
Hello @Vijay_Kumar_Kola 

Approval Workflow can be achieved with SAP Build Process Automation component. 

Reference Architecture for such scenarios are included in Discovery Center. 

 

Workflow Management was a former service that is now part of SAP Build Process Automation. 

The following setup is required to accomplish receiving events from S4 Public Cloud and process them as Workflow in SAP Build Process Automation. 

 

  • Subscribe and configure SAP Build Process Autamation
  • Subscribe and Configure Event Mesh
  • Enable Events and the respective event object in S4 Public Cloud
  • Configuration Destinations between each component. Between Event Mesh and S4 Public Cloud, the normal configuration can apply. 

See the following documents explaining this process. 

  • Setup for each component is described here.
  • Setup for Event Management in Project Overview in SAP Build Process Automation is described here.

Hope this information helps you understand how to configure all components to achieve the Sales Order Approval Process. It's a nice business scenario. 

If you are successful, share with us your success and findings. Would be great to read about successful use cases implementation using SAP Build Process Automation. 

Enjoy the challenge.

Tiago Ribeiro

entry.title: Re: Activate Job Template in S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Thank you for the information.

For information, the concerned Fiori apps are those referring to Application Job Catalogs with the checkbox "Scoping relevant" activated, which have been created via the transaction code SAPJ or via Eclipse ADT (they correspond to objects with an Object Directory Entry of type "R3TR SAJC").

The "Scoping relevant" flag is explained in the SAP Help Portal > Home > SAP NetWeaver Application Server for ABAP 7.52SAP Fiori Apps for SAP NetWeaver > SAP Fiori Applications > Application Jobs > Defining Job Catalog Entries in ABAP > Parameters on the Header Tab:

If a job catalog entry is defined as Scoping relevant, the application job component makes it available only in clients with a corresponding tuple (<client id>, <job catalog entry id>). These are maintained in the APJ_C_SCOPE table. If the flag is not set, the job catalog entry is available in all clients.

The maintenance is to be done via the transaction code SM30 > APJ_C_SCOPE > Maintain. Also, the SPRO activity can be reached via SM30 > APJ_C_SCOPE > Customizing > Project OR Continue w/o Specifying Project. The mention "tuple" is not very clear, it just means that you have to maintain the table APJ_C_SCOPE in each client where you want to run the Fiori App.

entry.title: FIORI Elements: Missing UI Message from Backend
entry.content:
Hi, 

i have created FIORI Elements App with BOPF as Backend. 

I implemented custom Actions, but triggered Error Messages from Backend are not displayed in FIORI but are visible in Network Console.

Backend Implementation:

 

  METHOD /bobf/if_frw_action~execute.

    DATA: usr_scenario_table TYPE Zt_pmc_usr_scen_ass.
    io_read->retrieve(
      EXPORTING
        iv_node                 = is_ctx-node_key
        it_key                  = it_key
      IMPORTING
        et_data                 = usr_scenario_table
    ).
    DATA(line) = usr_scenario_table[ 1 ].

    DATA: ls_message TYPE symsg.

    MESSAGE e000(ZPM_MO_US_MG) INTO DATA(lv_msg).
    MOVE-CORRESPONDING sy TO ls_message.

    ls_message-msgv1 = 'Errror in App'.

    IF eo_message IS NOT BOUND.
      eo_message = /bobf/cl_frw_factory=>get_message( ).
    ENDIF.

    eo_message->add_message(
      EXPORTING
        is_msg       = ls_message
        iv_node      = is_ctx-node_key
        iv_key       = it_key[ 1 ]-key
    ).

    APPEND VALUE #( key = it_key[ 1 ]-key ) TO et_failed_key.

  ENDMETHOD.

 

According to this UI5 Documentation there are two possible solutions for that:

  • Make Message Persistent
  • Defer read Operation after POST 

Topic 1: I have no idea how to make this message persistent, there is no parameter in the Message Object for that

Topic 2: I have set Attribute refreshAfterChange already to false in manifest.json file in BAS, Errors are still not displayed

 

Another User did have a similar Problem described here and solved it by addressing Importing Parameter "iv_message_target" to '/#TRANSIENT#'. But he did use another Message Container and probably had ODATA Service and not BOPF so its's not applicable for me.

Anyone got a hint?

EDIT:

After a bit of trial and error I tried deleting the selected line, Result was that i was successful and the List Report was automatically refreshed, so the above named attribute in manifest.json seems not to take effect in my Model.

SAP Fiori for SAP ERP

entry.title: SAP Machine Binary Machine and Similar Processes Maxing out Server Resources
entry.content:
Issue Description:

We are experiencing performance issues with our SAP Business One environment, specifically related to the SAP Machine Binary Machine process and other processes as shown in the screenshot image below. They collectively appear to be consuming excessive system resources, including CPU and RAM which is affecting overall server performance.

System Details:

  • SAP Business One Version: SAP B1 10.0 FP2405
  • Database: SQL Server
  • Server Specifications: CPU: 2.3 GHz(6 Processors), RAM = 16GB
  • Operating System: Windows Server 2019 Standard Evaluation
  • SAP Components Installed: SAP B1, Service Layer, B1 Web Client

Observed Behavior:

  • The SAP Machine Binary Machine process and other similar processes are collectively consuming high CPU and RAM usage, sometimes reaching close to 100% utilization.
  • Server performance degrades significantly, causing delays in overall operations, transaction processing, timeouts, and slow report generation.
  • Restarting the SAP services or server temporarily resolves the issue, but it returns after a while.

Troubleshooting Steps Taken:

  • Checked Windows Task Manager to identify resource utilization.
  • Monitored database activity to check for long-running queries or high load.
  • Disabled unnecessary services or background tasks related to SAP.
  • Increased server memory allocation, but the issue persists.
  • Upgraded SAP B1 to higher versions.

Any insights or recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: An Exception Was Raised – How Do I Fix This?
entry.content:
During SAP RAP application development, it’s common to encounter the error “An exception was raised.” However, figuring out the root cause of this error can often take days. This issue might occur when performing actions like clicking a button, opening an object page, saving a record, or during any random event execution.

Setting the Stage

To demonstrate how to identify and fix such errors, let’s take an example from a custom application I was working on. It’s a simple, unmanaged RAP application designed to create and update bank records in the standard table BNKA using the provided interface I_BankTP.

Below is how the list page and object page of the application look:

The update method in the application has the following logic:

 

 

* Changed record from transaction buffer
    DATA(ls_bank) = entities[ 1 ].

* Update the bank data
    MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_banktp PRIVILEGED ENTITY bank
     UPDATE FIELDS (
                     longbankname
                     longbankbranch
                   )
     WITH VALUE #( (
         %key-bankcountry    = ls_bank-BankCountry
         %key-bankinternalid = ls_bank-BankInternalID
         longbankname        = ls_bank-BankName
         longbankbranch      = ls_bank-BankBranch
                 ) )
      FAILED DATA(failed_bank) REPORTED DATA(reported_bank) MAPPED DATA(updated_mapped).

 

The logic simply takes whatever is passed in the importing parameter entities of the update method and uses it in the EML statement to update the bank record. When I change the Bank Name, the application works perfectly. However, when I try to update only the Bank Branch field, it throws an error.

Did I make a mistake in the logic?

The syntax is copied directly from the I_BankTP documentation. So, what went wrong?

Understanding the Error

To diagnose the issue, we can use a tool called - ABAP Cross Trace in the ABAP Development Tools (ADT).

  1. Open the ABAP Cross Trace Tool: Navigate to Window > Show View > Others in the Eclipse navigation bar.

  2. Create a New Trace: Start a new trace for the logged-in user.

  3. Configure the Trace Settings: Adjust the settings as needed or keep the default values. Click OK to activate the trace.

  4. Activate the Trace: Once activated, the trace will appear in the cross trace list.

  5. Execute the Event: Go back to the application and perform the action that triggers the exception. In this case, click the Save button on the object page.

  6. Deactivate the Trace: After the exception occurs, return to the trace and deactivate it.

  7. Review Trace Results: Once deactivated, a new tab called Trace Results will appear beside the Trace Configurations tab. Double-click on the trace result to open it.

  8. Identify the Failure Point: Expand the trace details to locate where the execution failed for the first time. In this example, the failure occurred in the process Check Before Save.

  9. Find the Error Message: Expand the process to locate the subprocess containing the error messages. You should find Failed messages. In my case, we have 1 failed message.

  10. Double-click on FAILED:1, REPORTED:2 to open the contents of FAILED and REPORTED. Check the REPORTED table for the error message ID and number.

  11. Analyze the Error: Upon checking the message class, we found the error. When editing a record and changing only the Bank Branch, the Bank Name field remains blank if it’s not updated.

  12. Root Cause: I debugged the code and validated the case. The application attempts to modify the bank records using the standard interface I_BANKTP with a blank Bank Name, which triggers the exception.

  13. Fix the Logic: To resolve this, modify the update method logic to ensure Bank Name is always populated:

 

* Changed record from transaction buffer
    DATA(ls_bank) = entities[ 1 ].

* Fetch existing data
    SELECT SINGLE * FROM zsac_r_bank
        WHERE BankCountry = @LS_bank-BankCountry
          AND BankInternalID = @LS_bank-BankInternalID
        INTO @DATA(ls_existing_bank).

* Update the bank data
    MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_banktp PRIVILEGED ENTITY bank
     UPDATE FIELDS (
                     longbankname
                     longbankbranch
                   )
     WITH VALUE #( (
         %key-bankcountry    = ls_bank-BankCountry
         %key-bankinternalid = ls_bank-BankInternalID
         longbankname        = COND #( WHEN ls_bank-BankName IS INITIAL
                                       THEN ls_existing_bank-BankName ELSE ls_bank-BankName )
         longbankbranch      = ls_bank-BankBranch
                 ) )
      FAILED DATA(failed_bank) REPORTED DATA(reported_bank) MAPPED DATA(updated_mapped).

 

  • Test the Fix: After making the changes, the application works as expected. This exercise was a great learning experience in using the ABAP Cross Trace tool to diagnose and resolve exceptions.

That's it folks!

entry.title: Job cancelled without reason
entry.content:
Hello community,

I have a specific program that runs in the background via a job, it is a process that takes several hours for an initialization phase, on average 16-18 hours (a few minutes later), several tests have been successfully performed for this initialization.

But for a few days new tests have been launched on the qualification server and the job is canceled by the system after about 8 hours of execution, no dump from the program (ST22).

The job logs say to go see the system logs (SM21); this is very system information, which I do not really know how to analyze.

Here is the data that I have in my possession from the Syslog log details view:

Message details
Message ID           Q02
Category             Operation trace
Message Text         Stops work process 43 (PID = 52430, Info = Exit with status 0)

Session details
Package              STSK

Technical details
SysLog type          m Error (Function,Module,Row)
variable data        wp_halthalt wpdpuxtool 334

Parameter details
Message
Function             wp_halt
Reason               halt wp
Program              dpuxtool
Line                  334

What can I do at my level as a developer? Should I check memory consumption?

Thanks in advance for your help.

entry.title: Re: Activate Job Template in S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Here is how to generalize your blog post to any standard Fiori App for which you don't see a Job Template proposed:

  1. Obtain the ID of the Fiori App via Profile button > About (e.g. F2823 is obtained in the case of "Product Classification for Demand-Driven Replenishment")

  2. Search the Fiori App ID in the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library (the following page is obtained in the case of F2823: Schedule Product Classification (DD))
  3. Go to the panel IMPLEMENTATION INFORMATION > Configuration, e.g.:
  4. Get the value of the parameter "JobCatalogEntryName" in the App Launcher, e.g.:
  5. Now you know the value to add to the SPRO activity "Activation of Scope-Dependent Application Job Catalog Entries and Maintain your Job Catalog" or more directly SM30 APJ_C_SCOPE ("SAP_SCM_DD_PRODUCT_CLASSFCTN" in the case of F2823)

entry.title: User Replication from Cloud Identity Service to SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
Pre-requisite: You should have an SAP BTP account and should have cloud Identity Service enabled.

Step 1: Log into your Cloud Identity Services account

Goto Identity Provisioning -> Source Systems

Step 2: Click on Add to add a new source system

Step 3: Goto Properties section and maintain the properties as shown below.

URL should be the cloud identity service URL.

Then Click on Save

Step 4: Goto Outbound Certificates Tab and then download the certificate using the download button as shown below

Step 5: Goto Users & Authorizations Tab and Navigate to Administrators.

Step 6: Click on Add -> System as administrator. Enter a system name and configure the authorizations by Turning on/off, then Click on Save

 

 

 

 

After saving, you should be able to see an option “Configure System Authentication”

Goto to Certificate and drilldown and upload the certificate which was downloaded from step 4 while configuring source systems and save it.

 

 

Step 7: Goto Identity Provisioning and select Target Systems

Click on “Add” to add a new Target system to which the users need to be replicated.

Type – SAP BTP ABAP environment

Source system:  Pick the source system which was created in Step 2

 

 

Goto Properties section and maintain the below details and the URL should be the BTP ABAP environment to which users are to be replicated.

 

 

 

 

 

Step 8:  Goto Outbound certificates and use the download option to download the certificate of Target system

 

 

 

Create a communication Arrangement in the Target ABAP system

Login into SAP BTP ABAP system

Step 9:  Goto Communication Management -> Maintain Communication Users

Create a communication user by maintaining User name and Description

And then Upload the Certificate which was downloaded for Target Systems at Step 8

 

 

Step 10: Create a communication system with system ID and System name and mark it as Inbound only

 

 

Navigate to Users for Inbound communication and add the communication user created above with Authentication method as SSL Client Certificate

 

 

Step 11:  Create a communication Arrangement using communication scenario SAP_COM_0193, select the communication system which was created above.

 

 

You can now go back to Cloud Identity service for replicating the users.

Goto identity Provisioning -> Select the Source System and then go to “Jobs” section

Click on Run and it would replicate the users from your Cloud Identity Service to SAP BTP ABAP Environment.
